body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: LEVER type + confidence rename
body.intention: I intend that the LEVER type and confidence rename are verified to produce more readable code and no retrieval regressions.
body.description:
**What changed:** Memory layer renamed `inertia` → `confidence`; added LEVER type (base 0.85); added source-seeded starting confidence.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Callers that string-match 'inertia' in log output break silently (grep logs for 'inertia' after ship)
- FACTUAL baseline bump (0.25→0.65) causes FACTUAL memories to crowd out EPISODIC in pre-inference assembly — check top-10 memories surfaced before/after
- Source multiplier defaults to 'unknown' (0.75) everywhere — effective change from flat baseline may shift retrieval ranking for existing memories
- table_inertia.py rename breaks any external script that imports it by name

**Signals to watch:** `grep -r 'inertia' devices/igor/ --include='*.py'` returns zero; pre-inference assembly output still contains a mix of types (not dominated by FACTUAL); no import errors after table rename.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-24.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe retrieval quality and naming correctness; annotate as clean / retrieval-skewed / callsite-missed
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
